ops.title: meshtrust fail-closed decisions (security-review dispositions)
ops.body: Extends cc-present note f31b124 (internal/trust fail-closed design) into the extracted synckit/meshtrust package. Three security-review dispositions (2026-07-17), all deliberate: (1) a registry naming no self identity trusts NOBODY — refresh() fails closed on reg.Self == "", because state.json can exist for other synckit features (or as {}) with no mesh configured, and Detect()'s file-exists probe alone must not grant self-tailnet-IP/self-MagicDNS trust; (2) Listeners()/bindTailnet refuses unspecified (wildcard) addresses — addrs are concrete tailnet host addresses (Provider.SelfAddrs); the unexported cc-present original could only receive SelfAddrs, and exporting it must not let a caller bind 0.0.0.0/:: under a loopback-intent bind; (3) current() never caches a refresh run under a dead caller context — one canceled caller must not stamp an empty snapshot fresh and deny every TrustedPeer/TrustedOrigin caller for the 30s TTL; genuine environment failures (registry/tailscale errors under a live ctx) remain TTL-cached per f31b124's retry-cadence decision. Don't 'fix' any of these toward partial acceptance or unconditional caching.
